title: hibbertia glomerosa
intro: Hibbertia glomerosa, also known as the sticky currant or yellow codon, is a small shrub native to eastern Australia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help soothe skin conditions and wounds. The health benefits of Hibbertia glomerosa include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial activity. Hibbertia glomerosa is often used in herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and salves, to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
